transcript:
Quick sync today (didn't catch the date). Bunch of us in the call - me, Khalifa, two of the design folks I think, maybe Reem? Not sure if Hassan joined.

So we kicked around the new AI assistant concept. Lots of ideas thrown around. Khalifa really likes the voice-first angle, but Reem thinks people will hate that. The design folks were torn. We sort of agreed voice should be optional but the default UX is still TBD.

We talked about pricing too. Maybe freemium? Or maybe team-based pricing? We'll figure it out later.

Someone (Khalifa I think?) said they'd put together a vision deck "soon" - I should follow up on that. And the design folks are going to mock something up by end of next week, possibly the week after if they're slammed.

We discussed integrating with the existing platform but didn't reach a clear conclusion. Reem wants tight integration, Khalifa wants it to feel like a separate product. We'll loop back.

One concern raised - if we go voice-first, do we need to worry about accent recognition for our regional users? Nobody really had an answer. Tabled it.

Action items? Honestly hard to say. Khalifa for the deck. Design for the mocks. Me to set up the next meeting.

We'll meet again soon to keep this moving.
